title.rendered: Top 10 New & Cheapest food delivery apps in India

content.rendered:
With the recent hit of the pandemic, more and more people have begun to realise that ordering food online is much more easy and convenient than going to a restaurant. This has opened up a wide range of opportunities for restaurants to go digital and reach a wider range of customers.

If you are a restaurant owner and you are looking forward to increasing the base of your business, then there is no better way than being a part of the food delivery apps in India. Sharing some commission, you can be part of the digital revolution in the food industry and make a fortune out of it. 

10 Best Food Delivery Apps in India [2022]

Now, you don’t have to keep searching high and low for the best food delivery apps in India. Here’s a list of the best and cheapest food delivery apps in India that the market of 2022 offers.

1. Zomato

Zomato is, without a doubt, one of the best online food delivery apps in India and has almost become synonymous with the same. It provides customers with detailed review of restaurants along with the current menu and food delivery options available. 

The company was founded by Pankaj Chaddah and Deepinder Goyal in the year 2008. The initial name of the company was FoodieBay, but was later renamed as Zomato in the year 2012. The company has its headquarters in Gurgaon, Haryana, India and has delivery locations all over the world.  Zomato mainly focuses on its operations in India but also functions in UAE, UK, Singapore and Lebanon as well. 

Restaurant owners have to pay anywhere between 18 and 25 percent as a commission to Zomato for being a part of their listing. The commission depends further on factors like the restaurant type, delivery cost and so on.

Founded Year: 2008

Headquarters: Gurgaon, India

Delivery locations: India, UAE, UK, Singapore and Lebanon

Commission for restaurants: 18-25%

2. Swiggy

Yet another popular and credible food delivery app in India is Swiggy and also offers on-demand delivery of groceries. This initiative of Swiggy is under the name Instamart and is quite useful for getting fresh supply of household products. They also offer instant package delivery service and is known as Swiggy Genie.

Swiggy was founded in 2014 and has its headquarters in Bangalore. Currently, Swiggy operates only in India and serves in over 300 cities across the nation. Restaurant partners have to pay 18 to 23 percent of their sales revenue to Swiggy for being a part of their system. 

Founded Year: 2014

Headquarters: Bangalore, India

Delivery locations: 300 cities across India

Commission for restaurants: 18-23%

3. Travelkhana

If you are someone who commutes frequently by train, then you must have heard of this food delivery app. Founded by Pushpender Singh in the year 2012, Travelkhana is a food ordering platform that provides food for passengers on Indian Railways. The company has its headquarters in New Delhi and functions actively all round the year. 

Travelkhana is available in over 500 cities across India and serves around 6000 trains on a daily basis. It would be an excellent way for restaurant owners to tap into the vast potential of the Indian Railway if they partner with Travelkhana. The commission ranges between 18 to 20 percent, which is quite reasonable and cheap. 

Founded Year: 2012

Headquarters: New Delhi, India

Delivery locations: 500 cities across India

Commission for restaurants: 10-20%

4. Box8

Just as the name suggests, Box8 is a food delivery platform that provides its food to the customers in convenient boxes. It makes the whole experience more hassle-free and easy. Founded in the year 2012, Box8 has its headquarters in Bangalore. They offer a variety of dishes including All-in-one meals, Desserts, Desi Boxes and even Desi Openers. 

The company currently serves in four cities, namely Bangalore, Gurgaon, Pune and Mumbai. Even when the locations that receive their service is limited, the company is said to serve almost over 1 million customers every month, which is an extremely good number.

Founded Year: 2012

Headquarters: Bangalore, India

Delivery locations:  Bangalore, Gurgaon, Pune, Mumbai

Commission for restaurants: You can contact them at partnership@box8.in for further details.

5. FreshMenu

When we talk about some of the new food delivery apps in India, it would be impossible not to mention FreshMenu in it. The company offers food delivery services in three cities, which are Delhi, Mumbai and Bangalore. The concept of dark kitchen or ghost kitchen is made use of by the company, and by partnering with them, you too can be a part of this. 

Founded in 2014 by Rashmi Daga, the company has its headquarters in Bangalore. The FreshMenu Affiliate Programme helps its members earn a payout of upto 40 rupees per sale, which is an excellent amount when compared to companies offering similar programmes. 

Founded Year: 2014

Headquarters: Bangalore, India

Delivery locations: Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore

Commission for restaurants: You can mail them at order@freshmenu.com for further details.

6. Eat.fit

The past few years have seen an increase in demand for clean and healthy food among millenials, and this is what sparked the idea of Eat.fit. Their menu usually consists of homely foods, snacks and dishes that are healthy and lean. 

Founded in the year 2016, Eat.fit has Cult.fit as its parent company. It has its headquarters in Bangalore and offers affiliate programs for individuals looking forward to partnering with them.

Founded Year: 2016

Headquarters: Bangalore, India

Delivery locations: Bangalore Hyderabad, Coimbatore

Commission for restaurants: You can mail them at hello@curefit.com for more details.

7. EatSure

EatSure is one of the new food delivery apps in India, founded in the year 2020 by Rebel Foods. It is an omni channel platform and customers can order from multiple restaurants at the same time. It is more like an online food court and this is a feature that is lacking in most food delivery companies in India. It is also one of the cheapest food delivery apps in India as customers can order the services without the expense of delivery fees. 

The company has its headquarters in Mumbai, Maharashtra and is available in over 75 cities across India including Pune, Delhi, Mumbai and Hyderabad. 

Founded Year: 2020

Headquarters: Mumbai, India

Delivery locations:75 cities in India including Pune, Delhi, Mumbai and Hyderabad. 

Commission for restaurants: You can mail them at goodness@eatsure.com to know more details.

8. Dunzo

Dunzo offers delivery of groceries, pet supplies, fruits and vegetables, medicines and other essentials to customers residing in major cities across India. Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Bangalore, Dunzo has grown exponentially over the past few years. 

It currently serves in eight cities in India, which are Bangalore, Delhi, Gurgaon, Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, Jaipur and Mumbai. The commission rate of Dunzo ranges between 15 to 30 percent and can vary depending on the size and nature of the order. 

Founded Year: 2014

Headquarters: Bangalore, India

Delivery locations:  Bangalore, Delhi, Gurgaon, Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, Jaipur, Mumbai

Commission for restaurants:  15-30%

9. Magicpin

When it comes to the list of online food ordering websites in India, Magicpin is an important member in it. The main point of attraction for the company is the coupons and points they offer, which can be redeemed at other online stores after shopping. 

Founded in 2015 and headquartered in Haryana, this company has helped tons of customers not only order food, but also save some money along the way. The services are available in Bangalore, Delhi, Gurgaon, Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, Jaipur and Mumbai as of now. The commission rate for the partnership ranges between 10 to 12 percent which is quite cheap and affordable for restaurant owners.

Founded Year: 2015

Headquarters: Haryana, India

Delivery locations:  Bangalore, Delhi, Gurgaon, Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, Jaipur, Mumbai

Commission for restaurants:  10-12%

10. Voosh

With the innovative idea of 10-minute food delivery in India, Voosh has managed to grab the attention of both customers and restaurant owners. One of the biggest advantages of choosing Voosh is that it offers an affordable commission fee to restaurant owners compared to other online food aggregators.

The company was founded in the year 2020 and has its headquarters in Bangalore. They currently offer their services in various cities across Bangalore and are quickly gaining popularity among the users. 

Founded year: 2020

Headquarters: Bangalore

Delivery Locations: Various cities across Bangalore

Commission for restaurants: You can email them at kitchens@voosh.in for further details.

Conclusion

Now that we have had a quick look at the top 10 online food delivery apps in India, it’s your turn to make your choice and decide on the food delivery apps that you want to partner with. Take into consideration the kind of food these apps deliver, the cities they operate in and the commission rate.

How should I make the most out of delivery apps as a restaurant manager or owner?

Partner with as many food delivery apps as possible. This will help you get maximum visibility and sales.

Wouldn’t it be overwhelming to manage all these food delivery apps at the same time?

Yes, it would be. So it is advised that you invest in a good online food/restaurant order management system like Foaps. 

With Foaps, you can manage all your orders from multiple food delivery aggregator apps in a single dashboard. Start your 30-day free trial now.
